name: Schwartz Theory of Basic Human Values
core_claim: Shalom Schwartz's claim is that there exists a finite, near-universal set of basic human values that are organised not as an unstructured list but as a MOTIVATIONAL CONTINUUM: values that express compatible goals sit adjacent on a circle, and values that express conflicting goals sit opposite. The original (1992) theory specifies 10 broad values — Self-Direction, Stimulation, Hedonism, Achievement, Power, Security, Conformity, Tradition, Benevolence, Universalism — arranged on a circle with TWO orthogonal bipolar axes: Self-Enhancement (Power, Achievement) versus Self-Transcendence (Benevolence, Universalism), and Openness to Change (Self-Direction, Stimulation) versus Conservation (Security, Conformity, Tradition), with Hedonism straddling Openness and Self-Enhancement. The refined (2012) theory subdivides these into 19 more narrowly-defined values around the same circle and adds two organising principles: a personal-focus vs social-focus split, and a growth/self-expansion (anxiety-free) vs self-protection (anxiety-based) split. For a narrative recommender the central claim is NOT about what drives enjoyment per se — Schwartz is a theory of MOTIVATION/IDENTITY, not of media affect — it is that any text, including a synopsis or a single sentence of dialogue, can be located on this values continuum, and therefore the VALUES A STORY ENDORSES OR INTERROGATES can be encoded as positions on two stable bipolar axes. The differentiator the task highlights — that LLMs can place text on the Schwartz continuum from a single sentence — is well supported by recent computational work and makes the framework unusually directly operationalisable compared with most narrative theories in this set.
descriptive_vs_experiential: Schwartz operates at a MOTIVATIONAL / VALUES level that is distinct from both the descriptive ('what the story is about') and the experiential/affective ('what it feels like') axes most other frameworks in this set occupy. It is closest to a thematic-descriptive layer in that it asks 'what does this work value / argue for / treat as good?' — but it is more abstract than topic: two stories about wildly different subjects (a heist and a war drama) can share the same values signature (e.g. high Stimulation + Self-Direction, low Conformity). It is explicitly NOT affective: a story can foreground Universalism while feeling either uplifting or bleak; valence and arousal are orthogonal to where it sits on the values circle. So Schwartz adds a VALUES/IDEOLOGY dimension to the taxonomy — what the narrative endorses, glorifies, or critiques — sitting between raw topic and felt experience. For two-person matching its interesting property is that it captures whether a work's moral-political centre of gravity (e.g. tradition and security vs openness and self-direction) resonates with or grates against a viewer's own values, which is a known driver of identity-expressive media choice.
key_dimensions: TWO LEVELS, both candidate vocabulary inputs. Level 1 (original, 10 values on two bipolar axes — the most operationalisable for a synopsis): AXIS A: SELF-ENHANCEMENT (Power = social status, control, dominance; Achievement = personal success through demonstrated competence) <-> SELF-TRANSCENDENCE (Benevolence = caring for the welfare of close others; Universalism = understanding, tolerance, protection of all people and nature). AXIS B: OPENNESS TO CHANGE (Self-Direction = independent thought and action, freedom, creativity; Stimulation = novelty, excitement, challenge) <-> CONSERVATION (Security = safety, harmony, stability; Conformity = restraint of actions that violate norms; Tradition = respect for and commitment to custom/religion). HEDONISM (pleasure, sensuous gratification) bridges Openness and Self-Enhancement. Level 2 (refined, 19 values around the same circle) subdivides these: e.g. Self-Direction-Thought vs Self-Direction-Action; Power-Dominance vs Power-Resources; Universalism-Nature, Universalism-Concern, Universalism-Tolerance; Benevolence-Care vs Benevolence-Dependability; Conformity-Rules vs Conformity-Interpersonal; plus Face, Humility, and Security-Personal vs Security-Societal. Two cross-cutting organisers in the refined theory: PERSONAL-FOCUS vs SOCIAL-FOCUS values, and GROWTH/self-expansion (anxiety-free) vs SELF-PROTECTION (anxiety-based) values. For HelpME2C the most usable handles are the FOUR HIGHER-ORDER quadrants and the TWO bipolar axes — they give a low-dimensional, validated coordinate system a story can be placed on.
cross_cultural_validity: The STRONGEST cross-cultural pedigree of any framework in this set, and the reason it is attractive. Schwartz's theory was explicitly built and validated cross-culturally from the outset (samples spanning 80+ countries with the SVS/PVQ instruments). The refined 19-value theory was validated by Schwartz & Cieciuch (2022) across 49 cultural groups (N = 53,472) and 32 language versions using the PVQ-RR: the questionnaire reliably measured 15 of 19 values in the vast majority of groups (two more in most), the theory-based measurement models fit in every cultural group, and a multidimensional scaling analysis 'perfectly reproduced the theorized order of the 19 values around the circle' across groups. The two bipolar higher-order axes are the most robust part — the quasi-circumplex STRUCTURE replicates near-universally even where the salience/priority of individual values shifts by culture. Caveats that matter for anime vs Western TV: (1) the MEANING of some values is partly culture-bound — Face and Humility were added in the refined theory partly to better capture East-Asian / Confucian-influenced value space the original 10 under-described, so the original 10 may slightly under-fit Japanese material; (2) priorities differ sharply (collectivist-leaning cultures weight Conformity/Tradition/Security higher), so a work coded 'pro-Conformity' may read as oppressive to a Western coder but as harmonious/virtuous to its source audience — the AXIS POSITION is stable, the VALENCE attached to it is not; (3) a few values (Tradition, Humility) show weaker measurement invariance. Net: the coordinate system travels exceptionally well; the moral judgement layered on top does not, which a recommender should keep separate.
empirical_grounding: Two distinct bodies of evidence. (A) PSYCHOMETRIC grounding of the theory itself is very strong: decades of large-N cross-cultural survey data (SVS, PVQ-40, PVQ-RR), confirmatory factor analyses, and MDS confirming the circular order (Schwartz 1992; Schwartz et al. 2012; Schwartz & Cieciuch 2022). This validates the STRUCTURE but is about persons' values, not about narrative enjoyment. (B) COMPUTATIONAL/NLP grounding for extracting values FROM TEXT is recent and directly relevant to the 'single sentence' claim: the SemEval-2023 Task 4 'ValueEval' / Touche 'Human Value Detection' shared task built a labelled corpus of arguments annotated with Schwartz value categories and benchmarked many systems; Yeste & Rosso (2026, arXiv:2601.14172) 'Human Values in a Single Sentence: Moral Presence, Hierarchies, and Transformer Ensembles on the Schwartz Continuum' demonstrates SENTENCE-LEVEL placement on the continuum using transformer ensembles, examining higher-order hierarchies and per-value imbalance failure modes; arXiv:2602.00913 studies whether Schwartz higher-order values help sentence-level detection via hierarchical gating; and JMIR Mental Health (2024, e55988) showed LLMs reproduce Schwartz value STRUCTURE with acceptable CFA fit for 9 of 10 values and human-like opposing-value correlations. CRITICAL GAP for HelpME2C: none of this validates Schwartz value labels against actual ENJOYMENT, COMPLETION, or PREFERENCE data, still less JOINT-viewing outcomes. The chain 'work's value signature -> a given viewer's enjoyment' is plausible (identity-expressive selection, value-congruence effects on persuasion/transportation are documented elsewhere) but is NOT established for streaming recommendation. To validate LLM-extracted Schwartz dimensions you would need: a corpus of works scored on the value axes (LLM labels checkable against trained-coder agreement, which the NLP literature shows is feasible at moderate F1), linked to receiver-side value profiles (PVQ-RR) and realised watch/enjoyment data, to test value-congruence as a predictor. [uncertain] Whether a work's Schwartz signature adds predictive lift over genre/theme baselines for recommendation is untested in the literature surveyed.
operationalisability: Among the MOST operationalisable frameworks in this set from a synopsis — its defining advantage. The NLP evidence (SemEval-2023 Task 4; Yeste & Rosso 2026; the JMIR LLM study) shows transformers and LLMs can place even a SINGLE SENTENCE on the Schwartz continuum, and that LLMs reproduce the value structure with acceptable fit. Practical recipe (mirroring how researchers do it): put all 10 (or 4 higher-order) value categories in ONE prompt and have the model return a graded score per value for the synopsis, rather than 10 separate classifiers. EXTRACTION UNIT: best at WHOLE-WORK / ARC level for a stable 'what does this story value' signature; sentence-level works in research but a synopsis is a summary, so document-level aggregation is the natural unit for our data. Distinguish two extraction targets that are easy to conflate: (i) values a story ENDORSES/glorifies (the hero is rewarded for loyalty -> pro-Conformity/Tradition), vs (ii) values it merely DEPICTS or interrogates (a story ABOUT a power-hungry tyrant who is condemned is not 'pro-Power'). FAILURE MODES: (1) Endorsement vs depiction confusion — the single biggest risk; an LLM may tag a critique of power as high-Power. Mitigate by extracting along a 'celebrates / interrogates / condemns' valence per value. (2) Protagonist-bias — coding only the hero's values and missing that the antagonist or the narrative frame embodies the opposite pole. (3) Synopsis thinness — marketing blurbs flatten moral complexity; a nuanced work reads as generic. (4) Cultural mis-valencing of Conformity/Tradition (see cross-cultural). (5) The original 10 under-cover Face/Humility-heavy material (relevant for anime). (6) Conflict-aware coding — because the axes are BIPOLAR, a work can be high on Self-Direction AND its synopsis still mention Security; the model must resolve which pole the narrative centre of gravity sits on. Recommendation: extract FOUR higher-order quadrant scores plus a per-axis polarity, each with an endorse/interrogate flag, at work level — this is robustly extractable; the 19-value granularity is likely too fine for synopsis data.
motivation_served: Schwartz maps most directly to IDENTITY EXPRESSION and value-affirmation motivations rather than to hedonic fun or eudaimonic being-moved per se — though it touches both: Hedonism and Stimulation values overlap the hedonic/sensation-seeking motive, while Universalism/Benevolence-heavy works align with the eudaimonic, meaning-seeking, 'being moved' motive other frameworks describe. Its real contribution is the IDENTITY/values-congruence channel: people prefer and are more persuaded by media that affirms their own values, and media choice is partly self-presentation. For GROUP viewing this is high-stakes and double-edged. On the constructive side, two people who SHARE a values quadrant (both Self-Transcendence-leaning) will likely agree on what feels 'good to watch together', and shared values predict comfortable co-viewing. On the conflict side, the bipolar structure means value disagreement is STRUCTURAL, not incidental: a Conservation-leaning viewer and an Openness-leaning viewer are positioned at opposite poles, so a work that strongly affirms one pole may actively alienate the other — this is exactly the kind of latent friction a two-person recommender should detect and route around (favour works near the centre / low on the contested axis, or works whose values are interrogated rather than preached). It also flags genuinely incompatible pairs where no single work satisfies both value identities, which is itself useful signal.
receiver_dependence: MIXED and this is the key design subtlety. A work's VALUE SIGNATURE (where its endorsed values sit on the two axes) is treated here as a property of the WORK — analogous to genre — and is reasonably absolute: an LLM can label 'this story glorifies loyalty and tradition' from the text with inter-coder-checkable reliability. In that sense it is more work-intrinsic than, say, Self-Determination need-satisfaction. HOWEVER, the thing that PREDICTS enjoyment is not the work's signature alone but its CONGRUENCE with the receiver's own values, which is purely relational: the same pro-Tradition work is affirming to a Conservation-leaning viewer and alienating to an Openness-leaning one. So Schwartz cleanly SEPARATES an absolute axis (the work's coordinates) from a relational axis (fit between work-coordinates and each viewer's PVQ-RR coordinates). For HelpME2C this is ideal architecture: store the work's value-quadrant scores as absolute content metadata (extractable from synopsis), store each user's value profile separately (from onboarding or revealed preference), and compute congruence/conflict at match time. The two-person case then becomes a tractable geometry problem on a validated 2D circle: find works whose coordinates minimise the maximum value-distance to both viewers, or that sit on the axis where the pair agrees. Unlike gore (absolute) or mood-fit (fully relational), Schwartz gives BOTH a clean absolute label AND a principled relational metric.
blind_spots: (1) NOT A THEORY OF ENJOYMENT OR AFFECT — it says nothing about pacing, suspense, thrill, comedy, fear, spectacle, or craft; a value signature does not tell you whether a work is fun, tense, or beautiful. Two works with identical Schwartz coordinates can differ totally in watchability. (2) ENDORSEMENT vs DEPICTION ambiguity is intrinsic, not just an extraction bug: morally complex art deliberately refuses to 'endorse' a pole, and the framework has no native slot for ironic, satirical, or interrogative stances. (3) AESTHETIC/FORMAL appreciation (nonlinear-storytelling, cinematography, meditation-on-time) is invisible. (4) PLOT MECHANICS and structure (mystery, heist, time-loop) are orthogonal — a whodunit and a war film can share values. (5) NEGATIVE/painful enjoyment (the tragedy paradox) is unaddressed. (6) Within-work value CONFLICT is the dramatic engine of most good stories (a character torn between loyalty and freedom), yet the framework's circle is built to describe a person's stable priorities, not a narrative's deliberate staging of value collision — it can locate the poles in tension but not the dramatic value of the tension itself. (7) It risks importing a moral/ideological reading where viewers just want escapism — over-coding values onto light entertainment. (8) Predictive validity for media SELECTION (vs measuring persons) is assumed, not demonstrated.
vocabulary_implications: Recommendation: do NOT add the 10/19 value names as theme slugs (they are motivational constructs, and 'Power' or 'Universalism' are not themes a synopsis is 'about'). Instead adopt Schwartz as a derived VALUE-SIGNATURE OVERLAY with a small, robustly-extractable surface: (A) Two bipolar axis scores per work, each in [-1, +1]: VALUE-AXIS-ENHANCEMENT-TRANSCENDENCE (negative = celebrates power/status/personal success; positive = celebrates care for others/justice/tolerance) and VALUE-AXIS-OPENNESS-CONSERVATION (negative = celebrates safety/tradition/conformity; positive = celebrates freedom/novelty/self-direction). (B) Per axis an ENDORSEMENT-STANCE flag {celebrates | interrogates | condemns} so a critique of power is not mislabelled pro-power — this is essential and is the main thing that makes the overlay trustworthy. (C) Optionally a FOUR-QUADRANT primary label for display ('a story that prizes loyalty and stability' vs 'a story that prizes freedom and self-expression'). NEW human-readable theme slugs justified by genuine gaps where the value content is also a recognisable theme: 'tradition-vs-modernity' (the Conservation<->Openness collision dramatised — extremely common and currently unnamed), 'individual-vs-collective' (personal-focus vs social-focus tension), 'duty-vs-desire' (Conformity/Tradition vs Hedonism/Self-Direction), and 'justice-for-all' / 'common-good' (Universalism endorsement, distinct from the existing political slugs). NOT EXTRACTABLE FROM SYNOPSIS and requiring other data: the USER's value profile (needs onboarding PVQ-RR-lite or revealed preference) and therefore value-congruence — flag these as RELATIONAL match-time inputs, never content tags. The work-side axis scores ARE extractable and should live on the absolute-metadata side.
relationship_to_existing_vocabulary: Schwartz cuts ACROSS most existing slugs rather than mapping one-to-one, which is its value — it provides an organising 2D coordinate system the current flat vocabulary lacks. VALIDATES and groups several Power/society slugs onto the Self-Enhancement<->Self-Transcendence axis: power-corrupts, class-conflict, legacy-of-empire, celebrity-and-fame sit on the Power/Self-Enhancement pole (usually interrogated/condemned), while revolution-and-rebellion, propaganda-and-truth tension and much of the moral cluster lean Self-Transcendence/Universalism. The relationship/emotion cluster (found-family, friendship-tested, platonic-soulmates, parent-child-rift) maps to BENEVOLENCE (Self-Transcendence, social-focus) — Schwartz explains WHY these cohere. The Conservation<->Openness axis is UNDER-SERVED by current vocabulary: there is no slug for tradition-vs-modernity, duty-vs-desire, or stability-vs-freedom, which Schwartz shows is one of the two master axes of human values — a clear GAP to fill (proposed new slugs above). SUGGESTS MISALIGNMENT / over-loading: politically-loaded slugs like revolution-and-rebellion and vigilante-justice conflate a values stance (Openness / anti-Conformity) with a plot topic; Schwartz suggests separating the value-axis position from the topic. STAYS SILENT on the entire Texture cluster (slow-burn, nonlinear-storytelling, magical-realism, character-study, meditation-on-time/death) and most Genre/structure slugs (mystery-investigation, heist-and-caper, time-loop) — confirming these serve craft/structure/affect motivations Schwartz does not model, and that a values overlay must be ADDITIVE to, not a replacement for, the existing thematic and structural slugs. Net: endorses the moral and relationship clusters as expressions of two master value axes, reveals a Conservation<->Openness gap, recommends de-conflating value-stance from topic in the power/rebellion slugs, and is correctly silent on texture/structure.
key_sources: Schwartz (1992) 'Universals in the Content and Structure of Values,' Advances in Experimental Social Psychology 25:1-65 — foundational 10-value theory (paywalled; widely available via library/ResearchGate). Schwartz, Cieciuch, Vecchione et al. (2012) 'Refining the Theory of Basic Individual Values,' Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 103(4):663-688 — the refined 19-value theory; FREE PDF: https://scottbarrykaufman.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/09/Schwartz-2012-19-values-JPSP.pdf. Schwartz & Cieciuch (2022) 'Measuring the Refined Theory of Individual Values in 49 Cultural Groups: Psychometrics of the Revised PVQ-RR,' Assessment 29(5) — key cross-cultural validation; FREE via PMC: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9131418/ (publisher: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1073191121998760). PVQ-RR coding instructions (FREE): https://www.researchgate.net/publication/308166496. Computational / single-sentence (directly supports the operationalisability claim): Yeste & Rosso (2026) 'Human Values in a Single Sentence: Moral Presence, Hierarchies, and Transformer Ensembles on the Schwartz Continuum,' arXiv FREE: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.14172; 'Do Schwartz Higher-Order Values Help Sentence-Level Human Value Detection?' arXiv FREE: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.00913; SemEval-2023 Task 4 'Human Value Detection' (Touche/ValueEval) shared-task corpus (search 'SemEval-2023 Task 4 ValueEval'). LLM-values alignment: 'Assessing the Alignment of LLMs With Human Values ... Using Schwartz's Theory,' JMIR Mental Health (2024) e55988, FREE: https://mental.jmir.org/2024/1/e55988; 'Do LLMs Have Consistent Values?' ICLR 2025, FREE: https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/68fb4539dabb0e34ea42845776f42953-Paper-Conference.pdf.
